role: Minister of Energy (2009–2013); EU-sanctioned for nuclear program links
bio:
Majid Namjoo is an Iranian politician who served as Minister of Energy in [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad)'s second cabinet from August 2009 to August 2013. He was confirmed by the [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) with 210 out of 290 votes.

As head of the [Ministry of Energy](/orgs/ministry-of-energy), Namjoo oversaw electricity generation and water resources during a period of increasing international sanctions pressure. In October 2012, the European Union placed him on its sanctions list due to his connection to Iran's nuclear program.

Namjoo succeeded [Parviz Fattah](/people/parviz-fattah) as energy minister, continuing the pattern of [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps)-affiliated figures holding the portfolio.
editorial_notes: EU-sanctioned Oct 2012 for nuclear links. User brief mentions IRGC affiliation and embezzlement conviction but neither confirmed in web sources — marked irgc_member false and omitted prison claim.
